heading: Burncluith vs Burnett Heads — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Burncluith, Queensland and Burnett Heads, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Burnett Heads has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Burncluith (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Burncluith or Burnett Heads safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Burncluith has a flood risk score of 39/100, while Burnett Heads scores 29/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Burncluith scores 40/100 for bushfire risk. Burnett Heads scores 40/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Burncluith or Burnett Heads?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Burnett Heads has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.98% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
